faqs.a: Premenstrual dysphoric disorder, or PMDD, is a severe, recognized condition tied to the menstrual cycle. In the week or so before a period, a person has intense mood and physical symptoms that ease soon after the period starts, and the pattern repeats cycle after cycle.

faqs.q: How is PMDD different from regular PMS?
faqs.a: PMDD sits at the far end of premenstrual difficulty and differs in degree and impact. The symptoms are severe enough to disrupt relationships, work, and daily life, so it isn't just bad PMS or something to brush off.

faqs.q: Is PMDD caused by abnormal hormone levels?
faqs.a: No. PMDD reflects how a sensitive nervous system responds to normal hormonal shifts, not to abnormal hormone levels, so standard hormone tests usually look normal. Tracking symptoms across a couple of cycles helps confirm the pattern.
